heaviest_rainfall.status: The most intense rainfall recorded took place on 09 November 2025, with precipitation totalling 47.89mm.  The rain fell at 24.6mm per hour at times over the course of the 144.8 hour storm.

heaviest_rainrate.status: The rain rate reached 34.3mm per hour on 02 September 2025, and resulted in 21.19mm of rain over 15.0 hours.  This is the highest rate recorded by our weather station.

longest_rain.status: The longest rain storm on record lasted 144.8 hours on 09 November 2025 when 47.89mm of rain fell and at one point was falling at 24.6mm per hour.

most_lightning.status: The highest recorded number of lightning strikes in a single day is {highest_count} on {highest_date} since records began on 11 April 2025.

storm.status: No storms are expected.  The last storm was observed on 18 July 2026 and continued for 1 day.

top_10_dry.status: A significant dry period is on-going and is ranked 1st for length since 23 September 2024.  So far, it has lasted 22 consecutive days beginning 03 July 2026.

top_10_rainy.status: The longest rainy period started on 13 January 2026 and lasted for 22 days.

cold_snaps.status: There is not an active cold snap.  There has been 3 so far this year, while the average is 3.7.

extreme.status: The largest temperature difference recorded was 27.7°C on 24 April 2026.  On that date the temperature reached 23.2°C and fell to -4.5°C.

heatwaves.status: You are currently experiencing a heatwave that started on 2026-07-01 and so far it has lasted for 24 days.  This year there has been 3 heatwaves, the average number is 1.9.

tropical.status: The most recent tropical night was on 28 June 2026 and this has only occurred 68 times since 1973.

average.status: The current average daily wind direction is south-westerly (227°).  In the UK, a south-westerly wind often means mild and wet weather.

gusts.status: The largest wind gust ever was on 27 January 2026 with a value of 128.2 mph.

run.status: 1737 miles is the largest wind run recorded on 21 October 2012.

speed.status: The top recorded wind speed is 93.7 mph on 13 October 2012.

top_10_windy.status: The longest windy period started on 11 October 2012 and lasted for 21 days.

summary.description: The past week and month had observable variation in light levels (down 4175 lux), cloud levels (up 487 m), and inside temperature (up 1.8°C) when compared to today.

summary.summary: Inside; temperatures will remain comfortably hot. Outside; sweltering, especially in sunny spots. Also, very sticky and muggy conditions are expected and winds will be practically absent. Showers, the sky’s high clouds will permit plenty of sunshine, and sunlight levels will peak at very high intensity. No significant shifts in the weather are expected.

washing.phrase: The conditions are favourable for drying your laundry outside right now but, as always, monitor the situation for changes to the weather.
